creator: raimeetravel
caption:
One of the best PTO-friendly international trips! I first visited nearly 10 years ago after finding $300ish RT flights from Michigan and just recently went back to celebrate the new year (which was a very fun time to be there!)

🇨🇴 I think 4 days is the perfect amount of time to spend in Cartagena if you only have a long-weekend… but if you do have more time, definitely explore other parts of Colombia like Santa Marta, Medellin & Guatapé, & Salento/Cocora Valley! 

For my map of fave restaurants/bars around the city: 

Comment LIME 🍋‍🟩 and I’ll send to you!

#cartagena #colombia #cartagenacolombia

transcription.text: One of the easiest international trips to take from the US is Cartagena, Colombia. So here's how to plan a perfect four-day weekend there. Flights there are pretty affordable and only three to five hours from most major US airports. Once you land, you'll spend your first day wandering around Old Town. These were some of my favorite places to eat and drink there. And whatever you do, do not miss the Getsemani neighborhood. It's a short walk away from Old Town. It's more local, more artsy, much more alive in my opinion. There's street art everywhere, live music at night, people dancing in the plaza. And honestly, some of my favorite food and bars were here. Like this iconic salsa place. You'll probably spend day two wandering around both of these neighborhoods again. Then on day three, you'll spend the day on a boat exploring the nearby islands. Here are some of the companies you can book this with and some of the best islands to visit and swim around. Then day four is chill day. Spend it at the beach, at your hotel pool. And when you're planning where to stay, this part is personal. You can stay right in the city center if you want to be in the middle of everything, but I personally love staying in Boca Grande. It's about a 20-minute walk into the Old Town and you get bigger hotels, pools, ocean views, and way more amenities. I love Colombia and Cartagena is such a great intro to a South America destination. Comment Lime and I'll send you my map of all my favorite places here.
